prompt:
Our social club runs bingo nights and the caller sheet disputes are getting silly, so I want a referee script that replays a night from files and prints exactly what happened. PowerShell 7, one file `bingo.ps1`, run as `pwsh -NoProfile -File bingo.ps1 -Cards <path[,path,...]> -Calls <file>` (the card list is ONE comma-separated argument, played in that order). Set-StrictMode Latest, ordinal comparisons, plain Write-Output lines, no Format-* anywhere.

A card file is 5 lines of 5 whitespace-separated tokens — rows of the printed card. Columns are B I N G O with the standard ranges per COLUMN: B 1-15, I 16-30, N 31-45, G 46-60, O 61-75. The center cell (row 3, col 3) must be the free spot `*`; `*` anywhere else, a wrong token count, a non-integer, or an out-of-range number make that line bad. A card's name is its filename without extension. Errors, stderr + exit 2: missing/extra args -> `usage: bingo.ps1 -Cards <card,...> -Calls <file>`; unreadable file -> `error: cannot read <path>`; bad line -> `error: <name>: bad card line <n>: <line>`; a number repeated anywhere on a card -> `error: <name>: duplicate number <v>`. Cards are validated in play order, then the calls file: one token per line like `N34` (blank lines and `#` comments skipped), letter must match the number's column range -> else `error: bad call line <n>: <line>`; the same token twice -> `error: duplicate call <token>`. Validate everything before printing anything.

The replay, all on stdout: first `CARD <name> ok` per card in order. Then per call, numbered from 1: `CALL <k> <token>`, then for every card holding that number (in card order) `DAUB <name> R<r>C<c>` with 1-based row/col. The free center counts as pre-daubed. After the daubs, check every card for a completed pattern. Pattern names and their priority when one call completes several at once: rows `R1`..`R5` top to bottom, then columns `C1`..`C5` left to right, then `D1` (top-left to bottom-right), `D2` (top-right to bottom-left), then `CORNERS` (the four corner cells). Each winning card announces only its highest-priority pattern: `BINGO <name> <pattern>`. The first call with any winner ends the night: announce every winner in card order, print `GAME OVER after <k> calls`, exit 0, remaining calls unread. If the calls run out instead: `NO BINGO after <k> calls`, exit 0.

Reference cards for the tests live in `fixtures/`. `pwsh -NoProfile -File test_bingo.ps1` is the acceptance contract — it pins whole transcripts including a round where two cards hit CORNERS on the same call and one where a single call finishes a row and a column together. Don't modify the test file or fixtures.
